notes: DEV-ONLY cheap probe. SINGLE variable changed from exp_024: SYSTEM_PROMPT_MATH gains an exact-fraction / no-round instruction (free-form only; MCQ prompt byte-identical). model_id, sampling, vLLM sizing all identical to exp_024 (pass-4 stage-1). split=dev (200 q) so the notebook runs only on data/splits/dev.jsonl and skips private inference + submission. Goal: test whether instructing the model to emit exact fractions / >=10 sig figs recovers the wrong_math PRECISION bucket (~50 cases on full public; right values judged wrong at 1e-08 tolerance due to rounding) WITHOUT regressing MCQ (exp_005 risk). Baseline for comparison is the 200-dev subset of exp_024's existing public_responses.jsonl (no new baseline run needed). exp_018 (0.628) is the hard floor; this never submits unless the dev gate passes and a full board run beats it.
